rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CRONIN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Volkswagen Group Of America¿S Motion For Recovery Of Expert Costs Pursuant To Ccp 998
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Volkswagen Group of America¿s motion for recovery of expert is granted. Defendant is awarded $91,697.42 in expert costs. The inclusion of release language in the 998 offers does not render the offers invalid. The offers were reasonable when made considering plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s specially-prepared discovery. While the amount of the expert costs is quite high, the costs were reasonable in amount and reasonably necessary to defendant¿s defense of plaintiff¿s claims. Perhaps the most significant issue is whether defendant¿s decision to file its summary judgment motion close to trial (and thus the court¿s ruling was not rendered until after defendant¿s incurrence of almost $100,000 in expert costs) is a basis to deny this motion in whole or in part. The court is unaware of any authority directly on point and believes that, since defendant would likely have been entitled to these costs had it prevailed at trial whether or not it had filed a motion for summary judgment, the timing of the motion for summary judgment is not a basis to deny any of the claimed expert costs. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CRONIN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ford Motor Company'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Per March 30,2011 Hearing)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Ford Motor Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (negligence and strict liability) and taken off calendar as to issues 3 and 4 (false representation and punitive damages). The declaration of Mr. Aahl, the deposition testimony of Mr. Rocker and Mr. Taylor and defendant¿s responses to GO 129 interrogatories create a triable issue whether Mr. Cronin was exposed to asbestos-containing brake and clutch parts attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the declaration of Mr. Aahl are overruled. While the excerpts of Mr. Aahl¿s deposition testimony provide significant impeachment of his statements in his declaration, the deposition testimony does not show that Mr. Aahl lacks personal knowledge for the statements he made in his declaration. Plaintiffs dismissed their claims for false representation and punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JEFF BRADSHAW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Sepco Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s D¿Amico objections to the declaration of Mr. Jimenez regarding the asbestos content of the materials he worked with at the Shell facility and the time frame he worked with Mr. Bradshaw at the Tosco facility directly contradict Mr. Jimenez¿s deposition testimony in his own case and the contradictions are not explained in the declaration. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES BARAGAR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Tax Defendant Gibbs Cox, Inc.¿S Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Plaintiffs¿ motion to tax defendant Gibbs Cox, Inc.¿s memorandum of costs is denied. The motion was filed after the deadline set by the parties. Although it is unfortunate that the parties¿ commendable meet and confer efforts resulted in a situation where the plaintiffs¿ motion is denied as untimely, on the current record the court lacks authority to relieve plaintiffs of the untimeliness. Although the parties requested that the hearing be continued from March 17 and then again from April 6, no further papers have been filed by the parties. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ORMAN SMITH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Air & Liquid Systems Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KIM MORGAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. After conceding that plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable issue regarding plaintiff¿s exposure to asbestos-containing insulation disturbed by defendant, defendant¿s reply memorandum states that the ¿sole issue ¿ is whether Dillingham owed a duty to warn¿ plaintiff about the hazards of asbestos. Defendant¿s argument that it lacks such a duty because plaintiff was aware of the hazards of asbestos-containing insulation and was able to recognize such insulation prior to the first time he was in the vicinity of defendant¿s employees disturbing asbestos-containing insulation is based on evidence submitted with defendant¿s reply papers. The court declines to consider such evidence because plaintiff did not have the opportunity to respond to that evidence in writing and such evidence was available to defendant at the time it filed its moving papers. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KIM MORGAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed March 8. = (220/HEK)
